Stage 1 Modeling Report

Inlet features → Effluent targets  |  Train: 2020, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024  |  Test: 2025  |  Run: 3

Summary

Model RF — Train RF — Test LR Baseline — Test
RMSECV RMSER² RMSEMAEMAPER² RMSER²
stage1_grab_BOD 11.608 17.782 0.329 3.459 2.553 26.2% 0.180 6.932 -2.293
stage1_grab_COD 37.068 50.041 0.195 23.737 19.997 62.5% -0.230 22.768 -0.132
stage1_grab_TSS 41.270 48.663 0.159 9.484 5.900 37.7% 0.024 16.287 -1.879
stage1_grab_pH 0.227 0.246 0.374 0.279 0.184 2.8% -0.638 0.268 -0.515
stage1_comp_BOD 13.910 13.972 0.423 3.469 2.495 26.3% -0.144 11.294 -11.126
stage1_comp_COD 48.312 44.612 0.268 22.393 18.441 68.6% -0.287 33.030 -1.800
stage1_comp_TSS 32.334 24.586 0.322 6.636 4.527 37.5% -0.391 21.412 -13.484
stage1_comp_pH 0.226 0.273 0.359 0.328 0.256 3.8% -1.691 0.317 -1.514

R² colour scale: ≥ 0.70 good 0.40–0.70 moderate < 0.40 poor

Key Observations

  • Best R² on test set: stage1_grab_BOD (RF R² = 0.180)
  • Worst R² on test set: stage1_comp_pH (RF R² = -1.691)
  • RF outperforms LR baseline (lower test RMSE) in 5 of 8 models.
  • All R² values are negative or near zero — expected at Stage 1. Inlet concentrations carry almost no signal for predicting effluent quality. Stage 2 (secondary clarifier features) will address this.
